title: T-001 — Lock reporting permission enum (rpt.* baseline) (12-REPORTING §3, §11)
prompt:
# T-001 — Lock reporting permission enum (rpt.* baseline)

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §3 (Personas & Roles + RBAC mapping), §11 V-RPT-ACCESS-*, §15 (Screens)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Append a delimited REPORTING_CORE permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering dashboard view, export (csv/pdf), preset save/share, schedule create (P2), settings admin, force-refresh, and integration-health admin. Permission enum is the single source of truth — Foundation T-046 ESLint enum-lock guard will block downstream module compile if these strings are missing. Per PRD §3 RBAC mapping the four reporting roles (viewer / operator / manager / admin) map to subsets of these strings.

## Implementation contract
1. Modify `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append `REPORTING_CORE_PERMISSIONS` constant block with JSDoc citing PRD §3 lines.
2. Export `ALL_REPORTING_CORE_PERMISSIONS` as a typed `Permission[]` so vitest can iterate.
3. Extend regex group to allow `rpt.<resource>.<verb>` flat-namespace (matches existing settings.* / npd.* pattern).
4. Modify `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — add RED test asserting presence of all rpt.* strings + regex + uniqueness.
5. Do NOT introduce per-resource matrix; flat dot-namespaced strings only (per Foundation [D2]).

## Files
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [modify]
- CODEOWNERS [verify entry — no edit unless missing]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the enum file is imported, when read, then exactly these strings exist: `rpt.dashboard.view`, `rpt.export.csv`, `rpt.export.pdf`, `rpt.preset.save`, `rpt.preset.share`, `rpt.preset.delete`, `rpt.schedule.create`, `rpt.schedule.run_now`, `rpt.schedule.delete`, `rpt.settings.read`, `rpt.settings.edit`, `rpt.mv.refresh`, `rpt.integration.read`, `rpt.rules_usage.read`.
2. Given a permission key matches regex `^rpt\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when the enum is parsed, then no duplicates exist (vitest assertion).
3. Given `ALL_REPORTING_CORE_PERMISSIONS` is exported, when its length is checked, then it equals exactly the count of new strings (14).
4. Given `CODEOWNERS` is read, when checked, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` remains under @architects ownership.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with the four ACs above. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` and confirm RED fails.
- GREEN: append the 14 strings + the typed array.
- Re-run vitest, run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the rbac package.

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce a parallel 4-level matrix model (rejected per Foundation [D2]).
- Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or routes.
- Do not remove or rename existing permission strings.
- Do not gate `rpt.export.xlsx` here — XLSX is P2 (PRD §4.2 #18); reserve namespace but do not add until P2 task.
- Do not include scheduled-delivery / external-bi flag-gated permissions in this baseline beyond schedule.create/run_now (P2 stubs, behind `reporting.scheduled_delivery` flag at the rule layer not the enum layer).

pipeline_inputs.description: Append rpt.* permission group (14 strings) to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts so downstream reporting tasks can guard pages/actions without violating the ESLint enum-lock contract.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §3 lists 4 reporting roles. Each maps to a subset of the 14 strings. Maintain dot-namespaced flat model. JSDoc each group with PRD §3 line refs. Reserve `rpt.export.xlsx` and `rpt.bi_embed.read` for P2; do not introduce them in this task. ALL_REPORTING_CORE_PERMISSIONS exported typed array. Permission enum is the SSOT — never accept Reference.RolePermissions table writes as substitute (Foundation T-046 enum-lock guard will fail compile).
out_of_scope: Do not seed Reference.RolePermissions in this task
Do not implement report_access_gate_v1 rule here (T-009)
Do not add P2 export.xlsx / bi_embed.* strings
Do not edit any UI files
acceptance_criteria: Given the enum file is imported, when read, then exactly these 14 strings exist: rpt.dashboard.view, rpt.export.csv, rpt.export.pdf, rpt.preset.save, rpt.preset.share, rpt.preset.delete, rpt.schedule.create, rpt.schedule.run_now, rpt.schedule.delete, rpt.settings.read, rpt.settings.edit, rpt.mv.refresh, rpt.integration.read, rpt.rules_usage.read
Given a permission key matches regex ^rpt\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$, when the enum is parsed, then zero duplicates exist (vitest assertion)
Given ALL_REPORTING_CORE_PERMISSIONS is exported, when its length is checked, then it equals exactly 14 and is typed Permission[]
Given CODEOWNERS is read, when checked, then packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts remains architect-owned
test_strategy: RED: extend packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with presence + regex + uniqueness + array-length assertions; run vitest, expect failure
GREEN: append strings; run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in packages/rbac
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a parallel 4-level matrix model (rejected per Foundation [D2])
Do not remove or rename existing permission strings
Do not add P2 strings (rpt.export.xlsx, rpt.bi_embed.read) — reserved namespace only
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or routes
Do not weaken Foundation T-046 ESLint enum-lock guard

No raw PII in reports/exports — PII columns (email, phone, address, surname, dob, national_id) must be redacted, hashed, or omitted at the read-model boundary. Consult Foundation T-117 pino redact allowlist for the canonical PII column set.
